title: myrsine athruphyllum
intro: Myrsine athruphyllum, also known as the Guinean boxwood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrsinaceae, native to tropical West Africa. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which may help to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Myrsine athruphyllum include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
